about.principles.observability.title: Observability as a feature:

about.principles.automation.title: Automation and reproducibility:
about.principles.automation.body: Eliminate manual toil and enable consistent outcomes.

caseStudiesPage.title: Case Studies
caseStudiesPage.subtitle: Real engagements with measurable outcomes. Industries include fintech, logistics, and cloud infrastructure.
caseStudiesPage.miau.title: Cloud migration & observability for a Lithuanian fintech
caseStudiesPage.miau.challenge: A fast-growing payments company was running on a monolithic PHP application hosted on bare-metal servers. Deployments took 4+ hours, incidents were invisible until customers complained, and the team had no staging environment.
caseStudiesPage.miau.solution: We migrated the core payment service to AWS (ECS + RDS), introduced Terraform for infrastructure-as-code, added distributed tracing with OpenTelemetry, and built a CI/CD pipeline in GitHub Actions with automated smoke tests on every deploy.
caseStudiesPage.miau.tech: AWS ECS · RDS · Terraform · GitHub Actions · OpenTelemetry · Datadog
caseStudiesPage.miau.result3: Zero production incidents in 90 days post-migration
caseStudiesPage.neonova.title: Custom ERP integration for a European logistics company
caseStudiesPage.neonova.challenge: The client operated three disconnected systems — a legacy ERP, a warehouse management tool, and a customer portal — with manual data entry bridging the gaps. This caused daily reconciliation errors and delayed invoicing by 3–5 days.
caseStudiesPage.neonova.solution: We designed and built an integration layer using an event-driven architecture (Kafka + NestJS), created a canonical data model to normalize records across systems, and delivered a real-time sync dashboard. Full contract test coverage ensured zero regressions during phased rollout.
caseStudiesPage.neonova.tech: NestJS · Kafka · PostgreSQL · Docker · Contract testing (Pact)
caseStudiesPage.neonova.result2: Invoice cycle time reduced by 4 days
caseStudiesPage.fueille.title: VDI rollout for a remote-first software consultancy
caseStudiesPage.fueille.challenge: A distributed team across 6 countries needed secure, consistent development environments. Engineers were losing 2–3 hours per week to environment drift, and sensitive client IP was stored on personal laptops with no audit trail.
caseStudiesPage.fueille.solution: We designed and deployed an Azure Virtual Desktop environment with per-team workspace templates, enforced via Terraform. Developer toolchains were containerized (DevContainers), secrets managed through Azure Key Vault, and access audited via Microsoft Entra ID conditional access policies.
caseStudiesPage.fueille.tech: Azure Virtual Desktop · Terraform · DevContainers · Azure Key Vault · Microsoft Entra ID
caseStudiesPage.fueille.result3: Full audit trail for all client IP access
caseStudiesPage.cta.title: Want results like these?
caseStudiesPage.cta.body: Every engagement starts with a focused discovery call to understand your context and identify the highest-leverage improvements.
pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.subtitle: Competitive Eastern European rates with Western-grade engineering quality. All engagements are outcome-focused with clear success criteria.
pricing.starter.name: Starter
pricing.starter.forWho: Best for: startups and small teams needing a focused audit or one-off improvement sprint.
pricing.growth.name: Growth
pricing.growth.forWho: Best for: scale-ups and product teams wanting ongoing engineering support and measurable velocity improvements.
pricing.enterprise.name: Enterprise
pricing.enterprise.forWho: Best for: larger engineering orgs requiring a dedicated team, architecture governance, and full-spectrum support.
pricing.faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
pricing.faq.q1: Do you offer fixed-price or time-and-materials contracts?
pricing.faq.a1: Both. Starter engagements are fixed-price with a clearly scoped deliverable. Growth and Enterprise retainers are time-and-materials with a monthly cap, giving you predictable costs and the flexibility to redirect effort as priorities shift.
pricing.faq.a2: We are open to discussing scope adjustments for longer commitments. For multi-quarter engagements, we typically offer a 10–15% discount. Non-profits and early-stage startups can contact us for reduced rates.
pricing.faq.a3: We invoice in EUR. For clients outside the Eurozone we can invoice in USD or GBP at the prevailing exchange rate. Bank transfer (SEPA and SWIFT) accepted.
pricing.faq.a4: Starter projects can usually begin within 1–2 weeks of contract signature. Growth and Enterprise retainers typically have a 2–3 week onboarding period to set up tooling, access, and working agreements.
pricing.faq.q5: What if we are not satisfied with the results?
pricing.faq.a5: Every engagement includes defined success criteria agreed upfront. If measurable targets are not met by the milestone review, we extend the engagement at no additional cost until they are.
pricing.notSure.title: Not sure which plan fits?
pricing.notSure.body: Tell us about your project and team size. We will recommend the right engagement model and scope — no hard sell, no obligation.
hero.calendly.aria: Book a free 30-minute discovery call via Calendly (opens in new tab)

socialProof.testimonial3.quote: Our distributed team across six countries needed a secure, consistent dev environment. The VDI rollout reduced onboarding from two days to 45 minutes. A genuinely transformative engagement.

exitIntent.title: Before you go—
exitIntent.subtitle: Get a FREE Code Health Audit report
exitIntent.description: We’ll send you a personalised report showing where your codebase scores on quality, test coverage, and reliability.
freeAudit.headline: Get a Free Code Health Audit Worth €2,500
freeAudit.subheadline: We review your codebase, identify what is slowing your team down, and hand you a prioritised remediation plan — at no cost, no obligation.
freeAudit.urgency: Limited to 5 audits per month — currently accepting applications
freeAudit.whatYouGet.heading: What is included
freeAudit.deliverable1: Architecture review — structural weaknesses, coupling issues, and scalability concerns documented with remediation priorities
freeAudit.deliverable2: Security scan — dependency vulnerabilities, exposed attack surfaces, and misconfigured access patterns
freeAudit.deliverable3: Performance bottlenecks — slow query patterns, blocking I/O, memory pressure points, and frontend bundle issues
freeAudit.deliverable4: Tech debt assessment — quantified complexity, duplication hotspots, and areas where velocity is eroding
freeAudit.deliverable5: Actionable report — prioritised fix list with effort estimates, delivered within 5 business days
freeAudit.forWho.heading: Who this is for
freeAudit.forWho1: CTOs and VPs of Engineering who suspect their codebase is slowing down their team
freeAudit.forWho2: Founders preparing for a fundraise or acquisition who need a clean bill of technical health
freeAudit.forWho3: Engineering managers inheriting legacy systems with no documentation
freeAudit.forWho4: Product leaders who keep missing sprint commitments and want to understand why
freeAudit.form.heading: Apply for your free audit
freeAudit.form.subheading: We will confirm availability within 1 business day.
freeAudit.form.name: Full name
freeAudit.form.privacy: No spam. We only contact you about your audit request.
freeAudit.faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
freeAudit.faq.q1: What does "free" actually mean? Is there a catch?
freeAudit.faq.a1: No catch. We conduct a genuine 5-day audit of your codebase at no charge. Our goal is to demonstrate value upfront. Some companies go on to hire us for a full engagement; many do not — and that is fine. We publish our findings regardless.
freeAudit.faq.q2: How do you access our code? Is it secure?
freeAudit.faq.a2: We work with read-only access to a private repository or a sanitised export. We sign an NDA before any code is shared. All findings are stored encrypted and deleted after delivery.
freeAudit.faq.q3: What tech stacks do you cover?
freeAudit.faq.a3: Our team covers Node.js, TypeScript, Python, Go, Java, .NET, React, Vue, and most SQL/NoSQL databases. If your stack is not listed, mention it in the form and we will confirm before you commit to anything.
freeAudit.faq.q4: How long does it take to get results?
freeAudit.faq.a4: We deliver the written report within 5 business days of receiving repository access. We then schedule a 60-minute walkthrough call to go through the findings with your team.
freeAudit.cta.title: Only 5 audit slots open per month
freeAudit.cta.body: Each audit gets our full attention. We cap intake to ensure quality. If you are curious about your codebase, now is the time to apply.
packages.title: Choose the Right Engagement
packages.subtitle: Five focused service packages — from a one-off audit to ongoing strategic leadership. All are fixed in scope and priced transparently.
packages.audit.name: Code Health Audit
packages.audit.idealFor: Ideal for: teams that need an objective assessment before committing to a larger engagement or ahead of a fundraise.
packages.sprint.name: Sprint-in-a-Box
packages.sprint.idealFor: Ideal for: founders and product teams who need a working MVP or feature shipped in 2 weeks without hiring a full team.
packages.retainer.name: Engineering Partner Retainer
packages.retainer.idealFor: Ideal for: growing companies that need ongoing senior engineering capacity without the overhead of a full-time hire.
packages.cicd.name: CI/CD Accelerator
packages.cicd.idealFor: Ideal for: teams spending hours on manual deployments, flaky pipelines, or struggling with slow feedback loops.
packages.cicd.include2: Automated build, test, and deployment setup
packages.cto.name: Fractional CTO
packages.cto.idealFor: Ideal for: early-stage startups and scale-ups needing strategic technical leadership without a full-time C-suite cost.
packages.unsure.heading: Not sure which package fits?
packages.unsure.body: Most clients start with a Code Health Audit. It surfaces the real priorities and often makes the next step obvious. If you already know what you need, book a 30-minute discovery call and we will confirm fit.
packages.compare.heading: Quick comparison
packages.compare.cicd.for: Slow deploys, broken pipelines, manual releases
packages.compare.cto.for: Early-stage startups, post-seed to Series A
sprint.headline: Ship Your MVP in 2 Weeks. €4,800 Fixed Price.
sprint.subheadline: A focused, self-contained development sprint. You bring the problem statement. We bring a senior engineer, a clear process, and tested code delivered in 10 working days.
sprint.deliverables.heading: What you get in 2 weeks
sprint.deliverable1: A scoped, working feature or MVP — tested, deployed to staging, ready for review
sprint.deliverable2: Clean, documented code with test coverage on critical paths
sprint.deliverable3: Daily async updates so you always know where things stand
sprint.deliverable4: End-of-sprint demo call and full code handoff
sprint.deliverable5: 30 days of post-sprint support for bugs or deployment questions
sprint.process.heading: The process
sprint.process.step1.title: Discovery call
sprint.process.step1.body: 30 minutes. We learn what you need built, what success looks like, and whether a 2-week sprint is the right fit. No pitch, no obligation.
sprint.process.step2.title: Scope alignment
sprint.process.step2.body: We send a concise written scope within 24 hours. You review, we adjust if needed, and then sign off. We only start once both sides are aligned on what done looks like.
sprint.process.step3.title: The sprint
sprint.process.step3.body: 10 working days of focused delivery. You get a private Slack channel or Linear project, daily progress notes, and can ask questions any time. We are heads-down building.
sprint.process.step4.title: Demo and handoff
sprint.process.step4.body: We demo the work on a live call, walk through the code, and hand over everything — repo access, environment config, runbook. You own it completely.
sprint.requirements.heading: What we need from you
sprint.requirements.body: A sprint works well when both sides are aligned from the start. This is the minimum we need:
sprint.req1: A clear problem statement — you know what you want to exist at the end of the 2 weeks
sprint.req2: A named decision-maker who can answer questions within the same business day
sprint.req3: Access to any existing code, design files, or infrastructure we will be building on
sprint.req4: Availability for the 30-minute discovery call and the end-of-sprint demo
sprint.midcta.title: Book a 30-minute discovery call
sprint.midcta.body: We will listen to what you need, tell you honestly whether a 2-week sprint is the right fit, and confirm pricing. No sales process.
sprint.faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
sprint.faq.q1: What happens if we do not finish everything in 2 weeks?
sprint.faq.a1: We scope conservatively to make sure we deliver on time. If the sprint ends and something agreed in scope is not done, we complete it at no extra charge. If you want to add scope mid-sprint, we either defer it to a second sprint or discuss extending the timeline together.
sprint.faq.q2: Is €4,800 really the full price? What is not included?
sprint.faq.a2: The fixed price covers two weeks of focused development and the demo handoff. It does not include third-party service costs (hosting, APIs, subscriptions) or work added outside the agreed scope. Everything else is included.
sprint.faq.q3: What kinds of things can you build in 2 weeks?
sprint.faq.a3: Authentication and onboarding flows, CRUD dashboards, integrations with external APIs, data pipelines, internal tooling, landing pages with forms, refactoring a specific module, or adding a test suite to an existing service. If it is well-scoped, 2 weeks is plenty.
sprint.faq.a4: Yes, and many clients do. Each sprint is scoped and priced independently. Repeat clients get priority scheduling.
sprint.faq.a5: Yes. We sign a mutual NDA before any project details or code are shared. Standard practice on every engagement.
sprint.bottomcta.title: Ship something real in 2 weeks.
sprint.bottomcta.body: Fixed price. Fixed timeline. No retainer required. Book the call, confirm scope, and we start the following Monday.
blog.codeHealth.title: How to Know When Your Codebase Is Slowing Down Your Business
blog.codeHealth.excerpt: Technical debt compounds silently. Learn the symptoms of an unhealthy codebase — and how a structured code health audit surfaces what internal reviews miss.
blog.sprint.title: What to Expect From a 2-Week Software Sprint (And When It Makes Sense)
blog.sprint.excerpt: A two-week sprint done right can ship a real, tested feature. Done wrong, it disappoints. Here is how to tell the difference — and what the MSS Sprint-in-a-Box process looks like.
packages.ai.heading: We also ship AI into production
packages.ai.body: We help AI startups and product teams ship models into production — from RAG pipelines and LLM-powered features to embedding search and AI-assisted workflows. If you are building with AI and need engineering support, talk to us.
codeAudit.headline: Code Audit — Find Out Why Your Dev Team Is Slow
codeAudit.subheadline: An independent, structured review of your codebase that surfaces the root causes of slow delivery, fragile deployments, and mounting technical debt.
codeAudit.areas.heading: Six areas we examine
codeAudit.area1.title: Architecture
codeAudit.area1.body: Coupling analysis, dependency mapping, modularity assessment, and scalability readiness.
codeAudit.area2.title: Testing
codeAudit.area2.body: Coverage gaps, flaky tests, missing integration tests, and mutation testing opportunities.
codeAudit.area3.title: CI/CD
codeAudit.area3.body: Pipeline efficiency, build times, deployment safety, rollback readiness, and environment parity.
codeAudit.area4.title: Security
codeAudit.area4.body: Dependency vulnerabilities, authentication patterns, data exposure risks, and OWASP compliance.
codeAudit.area5.title: Performance
codeAudit.area5.body: Slow queries, N+1 patterns, memory leaks, bundle size issues, and frontend rendering bottlenecks.
codeAudit.area6.title: Maintainability
codeAudit.area6.body: Code complexity hotspots, duplication, documentation gaps, and onboarding friction points.
codeAudit.deliverables.heading: What you get
codeAudit.deliverable1: Detailed written report with findings organised by severity and impact
codeAudit.deliverable2: Prioritised remediation roadmap with effort estimates
codeAudit.deliverable4: 1-hour walkthrough call to discuss findings with your team
codeAudit.pricing.title: €2,500 — one-time investment
codeAudit.pricing.body: Includes the full six-area review, written report with prioritised remediation roadmap, and a 1-hour walkthrough call. No recurring costs, no hidden fees.
codeAudit.pricing.free: Not sure yet? Book a free 15-minute architecture review to see if an audit is the right fit.
codeAudit.faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
codeAudit.faq.q1: How long does the audit take?
codeAudit.faq.a1: We deliver the full report within 5 business days of receiving repository access. The 1-hour walkthrough call is scheduled within the following week.
codeAudit.faq.a2: Read-only access to your repository (GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket). We sign an NDA before any code is shared. All data is encrypted and deleted after delivery.
codeAudit.faq.a3: You own the report and roadmap completely. Many clients use it to prioritise their next quarter of engineering work. If you want help implementing the recommendations, we can discuss a Sprint-in-a-Box or Retainer engagement.
codeAudit.faq.q4: What tech stacks do you cover?
codeAudit.faq.a4: Node.js, TypeScript, Python, Go, Java, .NET, React, Vue, Next.js, and most SQL/NoSQL databases. If your stack is not listed, mention it in the call and we will confirm coverage.
codeAudit.bottomcta.title: Stop guessing. Start with data.
codeAudit.bottomcta.body: A code audit gives you an objective, prioritised view of what is actually slowing your team down — and exactly what to fix first.
fractionalCTO.headline: Fractional CTO — Technical Leadership Without the €120K Salary
fractionalCTO.subheadline: Senior technical leadership on a flexible, part-time basis. Get architecture decisions, team guidance, and investor-ready strategy — without a full-time C-suite cost.
fractionalCTO.resp.heading: What a Fractional CTO does
fractionalCTO.resp1.title: Architecture decisions
fractionalCTO.resp1.body: Evaluate trade-offs, choose the right stack, and design systems that scale with your business.
fractionalCTO.resp2.title: Team hiring and mentoring
fractionalCTO.resp2.body: Define engineering roles, run technical interviews, and build a culture of quality and ownership.
fractionalCTO.resp3.title: Vendor and tooling selection
fractionalCTO.resp3.body: Cut through vendor noise. Pick the right infrastructure, APIs, and services without over-engineering.
fractionalCTO.resp4.title: Technical strategy
fractionalCTO.resp4.body: Align technology decisions with business goals. Build roadmaps that balance speed with sustainability.
fractionalCTO.resp5.title: Investor tech due diligence
fractionalCTO.resp5.body: Prepare your tech stack and documentation for investor scrutiny. Present architecture and risk clearly.
fractionalCTO.scenarios.heading: When you need a Fractional CTO
fractionalCTO.scenario1: Between CTOs — you need strategic continuity while searching for a full-time hire
fractionalCTO.scenario2: Pre-CTO hire — you need someone to define the role, set technical direction, and help you hire well
fractionalCTO.scenario3: Scaling past MVP — your product works but the architecture will not survive 10x growth
fractionalCTO.scenario4: Preparing for a fundraise — investors will ask hard technical questions and you need clear answers
fractionalCTO.compare.heading: Fractional CTO vs full-time CTO
fractionalCTO.midcta.title: Book a discovery call
fractionalCTO.midcta.body: We will discuss your current challenges, technical goals, and whether a fractional engagement is the right fit. No obligation.
fractionalCTO.faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
fractionalCTO.faq.q1: How many hours per week do I get?
fractionalCTO.faq.a1: The base engagement includes 4 hours per week of dedicated availability — enough for a weekly strategy session, async Slack access, and ad-hoc architectural decisions. Higher-intensity engagements are available.
fractionalCTO.faq.q2: Can you work alongside our existing CTO?
fractionalCTO.faq.a2: Yes. Some clients bring us in as a technical advisor or sounding board for their existing leadership. We adapt to the role that adds the most value.
fractionalCTO.faq.q3: What if we decide to hire a full-time CTO?
fractionalCTO.faq.a3: We help you define the role, write the job spec, evaluate candidates, and ensure a smooth handover. A clean transition is part of the service.
fractionalCTO.bottomcta.title: Technical leadership — on your terms.
fractionalCTO.bottomcta.body: Month-to-month. No long-term contract. Senior leadership from day one.
hireDev.headline: Hire a Senior Developer — Without the 3-Month Hiring Process
hireDev.subheadline: Outsource development to senior engineers in Lithuania and Europe. Start shipping production-ready code in days — not months.
hireDev.reasons.heading: Why outsource to MSS
hireDev.reason1.title: Start in days, not months
hireDev.reason1.body: Skip the 3-month hiring pipeline. Our senior engineers are available to start within 1-2 weeks of signing.
hireDev.reason2.title: Senior-level from day one
hireDev.reason2.body: No juniors, no ramp-up. Every engineer has 5+ years of production experience and a track record of shipping.
hireDev.reason3.title: No recruitment fees
hireDev.reason3.body: Hiring agencies charge 20-30% of annual salary. With us, you pay for engineering output — not a finder's fee.
hireDev.reason4.title: Flexible commitment
hireDev.reason4.body: Scale up or down month-to-month. No long-term lock-in. Pause or stop when the project is done.
hireDev.stack.heading: Tech stack
hireDev.stack.body: Our engineers specialise in the modern web stack. If your project uses these technologies, we can hit the ground running.
hireDev.engagements.heading: Engagement models
hireDev.engagement1.name: Sprint-in-a-Box
hireDev.engagement1.desc: A focused 2-week delivery sprint. Ideal for MVPs, proof-of-concepts, or shipping a specific feature fast.
hireDev.engagement2.name: Engineering Partner Retainer
hireDev.engagement2.desc: A dedicated senior engineer, 3-5 days per week. Ongoing delivery, code review, architecture guidance, and async access.
hireDev.bottomcta.title: Ship faster with senior engineers.
hireDev.bottomcta.body: No recruitment fees. No ramp-up. Production-ready code from senior engineers who have shipped before.
advisory.headline: CTO-in-a-Box — Technical Leadership Without the €120K Salary
advisory.subheadline: Async advisory for startup founders who need senior engineering guidance but aren’t ready for a full-time hire.
advisory.cta.primary: Start with a free intro call
advisory.includes.heading: What you get
advisory.include1: 4 async Loom video reviews per month (any technical question)
advisory.include3: Slack/email access for quick questions (24hr response SLA)
advisory.include4: Vendor and tool evaluation when needed
advisory.perfectFor.heading: Perfect for
advisory.perfectFor1: Pre-seed/seed founders making technical decisions without a CTO
advisory.perfectFor2: Non-technical founders who need a technical sanity check
advisory.perfectFor3: Small teams outgrowing their initial architecture
advisory.perfectFor4: Companies between CTOs or waiting for a senior hire
advisory.process.heading: How it works
advisory.process.step1.title: Book an intro call (free, 15 min)
advisory.process.step1.body: We learn about your product, stack, and the technical decisions keeping you up at night. No commitment.
advisory.process.step2.title: Share your context
advisory.process.step2.body: Product overview, tech stack, team structure, and current challenges. We get up to speed fast.
advisory.process.step3.title: Get ongoing guidance
advisory.process.step3.body: Record a Loom when a question comes up, get a detailed video response within 24 hours. Plus a monthly strategy call.
advisory.compare.heading: How it compares
advisory.faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
advisory.faq.q1: What kind of questions can I ask?
advisory.faq.a1: Anything technical: architecture decisions, hiring advice, vendor selection, security concerns, scaling strategy, code reviews, build vs buy — whatever you are wrestling with.
advisory.faq.q2: How do the async reviews work?
advisory.faq.a2: Record a Loom (or similar) explaining your question or showing the code/architecture in question. You get a detailed video response within 24 hours walking through our analysis and recommendation.
advisory.faq.q3: Can I upgrade to a full retainer later?
advisory.faq.a3: Yes. CTO-in-a-Box clients get priority access to the Engineering Partner Retainer when they are ready to scale up.
advisory.faq.q4: What if I need more than 4 reviews in a month?
advisory.faq.a4: Additional reviews are available at €100 each, or you can upgrade to the Engineering Partner Retainer for unlimited access.
advisory.bottomcta.title: Senior technical guidance for €500/month.
advisory.bottomcta.body: No hiring risk. No long-term contracts. Cancel anytime. Book a free 15-minute intro call and see if it is a fit.
advisory.bottomcta.cta: Start with a free intro call
scanner.headline: Website Health Scanner
scanner.subheadline: Analyze any website for performance, security, SEO, and accessibility issues. Get actionable insights in seconds.
scanner.progress.performance: Running performance analysis (this may take 30-60 seconds)...
scanner.perf.title: Performance
scanner.perf.desc: Core Web Vitals measure real-world user experience. Google uses these as ranking signals.
scanner.security.title: Security
scanner.security.desc: Security headers protect against common web attacks like XSS, clickjacking, and data injection.
scanner.seo.title: SEO
scanner.seo.desc: SEO score reflects how well the page follows search engine optimization best practices.
scanner.seo.good: Strong SEO foundation. Well optimized for search engines.
scanner.seo.ok: Decent SEO but there is room for improvement.
scanner.seo.poor: Significant SEO issues found. This site may struggle to rank.
scanner.a11y.title: Accessibility
scanner.a11y.desc: Accessibility ensures your website is usable by everyone, including people with disabilities.
scanner.a11y.good: Good accessibility. Most users should have a smooth experience.
scanner.a11y.ok: Some accessibility issues found that should be addressed.
scanner.a11y.poor: Serious accessibility barriers detected. Many users may be excluded.
scanner.tech.title: Detected Technologies
scanner.tech.desc: Frameworks and libraries detected from the page source.
scanner.cta.title: Want a deeper analysis?
scanner.cta.body: Book a free 15-minute expert review with Maxwell Software Solutions. We will walk through your results and identify the highest-impact fixes.
scanner.how.heading: What we check
scanner.how.perf.title: Performance
scanner.how.perf.desc: Core Web Vitals — LCP, TBT, CLS, and FCP. Powered by Google PageSpeed Insights.
scanner.how.security.title: Security
scanner.how.security.desc: SSL certificate and 6 critical security headers that protect against common attacks.
scanner.how.seo.title: SEO
scanner.how.seo.desc: Search engine optimization audit covering meta tags, structure, and best practices.
scanner.how.a11y.title: Accessibility
scanner.how.a11y.desc: WCAG compliance check ensuring your site is usable by everyone.
codeHealth.headline: Code Health Score
codeHealth.subheadline: Answer 15 quick questions about your engineering practices. Get an instant score with a breakdown across testing, deployment, security, and more.
codeHealth.progress.question: Question
codeHealth.q1: What is your test coverage level?
codeHealth.q2: How often do you deploy to production?
codeHealth.q3: What is your average PR review time?
codeHealth.q4: How often do you update dependencies?
codeHealth.q5: How many production incidents per month?
codeHealth.q6: How long does it take to onboard a new developer?
codeHealth.q7: How many people understand the auth system?
codeHealth.q8: Do you have a CI/CD pipeline?
codeHealth.q9: What is your monitoring and alerting setup?
codeHealth.q10: What is your database migration strategy?
codeHealth.q11: What is the state of your API documentation?
codeHealth.q12: How consistent is your error handling?
codeHealth.q13: When was your last security audit?
codeHealth.q14: How do you track technical debt?
codeHealth.q14.o3: A backlog exists but is rarely prioritised
codeHealth.q14.o4: Tracked in backlog with periodic cleanup
codeHealth.q14.o5: Scheduled debt sprints with measurable targets
codeHealth.q15: What does your code review process look like?
codeHealth.results.title: Code Health Score
codeHealth.tier.critical.desc: Your codebase is a ticking time bomb. Critical risks across testing, deployment, and maintainability need urgent attention before they cause a major incident.
codeHealth.tier.concerning.desc: Significant risks that will slow you down. Your team is likely spending too much time fighting fires instead of shipping features. Targeted improvements can have a big impact.
codeHealth.tier.healthy.desc: Good foundation with room to improve. Your practices are solid in some areas but have gaps that could become problems at scale. A focused audit would help prioritise the next steps.
codeHealth.tier.excellent.desc: Your engineering practices are strong. You have mature processes across testing, deployment, and observability. A professional review can help you go from good to exceptional.
codeHealth.cta.title: Want a professional deep-dive?
codeHealth.cta.body: Book a free 15-minute architecture review. We will look at your actual codebase and give you a prioritised action plan — no obligation.
